topical_tags.term: Miriam – death by divine kiss
Misas Tzadikkim – death of righteous as atonement
Para Aduma – red heifer atonement
Bigdei Kehuna – priestly garments as atonement
Mita Chatufa – snatched death (sudden)
Mita Dchufa – rushed death (one day illness)
Mita Mageifa – plague death
Kares – shortened lifespan (50-60 years)
Mita Bidei Shamayim – death by divine hands (natural)
Gematria – numerical value (bakelach = 60)
Mazal – fortune, luck, not dependent on merit
Rava vs. Rav Chisda – contrast of righteous individuals
Malach HaMavet – Angel of Death
Truma – priestly portion sanctity as protection
Meanos – joint wailing response
Metapchos – slapping in mourning
Kina – responsive funeral dirge
Shnitziv women – source of responsive wailing examples
Rabbi Yishmael – four elders console after sons' death
Kal Vachomer – if not, how much more so argument
Tzidkiyahu – king blinded yet died in peace
Yoshiyahu – died shot with arrows yet in peace
Consoler etiquette – wait for mourner to speak
Avel yoshev berosh – mourner sits at head
Chatan – bridegroom sits at head
Leich l'shalom – go in peace (to the dead)
Leich leshalom – go towards peace (to the living)
Yetziat Neshama – difficulty of soul's departure
Talmidei Chachamim – scholars always ascending
